sections.paragraph:
(from the root קָוָה), [once מִקְוֵח, once מִקְוֵא כ׳].

(1) prop. expectation, hope, confidence, 1Ch 29:15 Ezr 10:2 also the person confided in, used of God, Jer 14:8 17:13 50:7 .

(2) a congregation, gathering together (from the root קָוָה Niph. to be gathered together).-(a) of water, Gen 1:10 Exo 7:19 Levit. 11:36 .-(b) a host, a company of men and animals, as horses. Thus I understand the words, 1Ki 10:28, in explaining which most interpreters have differed widely (see Bochart, Hieroz. t. i. p. 171, 172; Michaeëlis in Suppl. page 2171, and on the Mosaic Law, vol. iii. p. 332), and this of late has been approved of by De Wette (Vers. Germ. ed. 2), וּמִקְוֵה סֹחֲרֵי הַמֶּלֶךְ יִקְחוּ מִקְוֵה בִּמְחִיר “and the company of the royal merchants (out of Egypt) took the troop (of horses) at a price.” There is a play of words in the double use of the word מִקְוֶה as applied to the company of merchants, and to the troop of horses. I now see that it was formerly so rendered by Piscator and Vatablus.
